content.text:
{Deep calls to deep at the roar of your waterfalls.}{Psalm 42:7}
 {The SVD is the most important factorization in numerical linear algebra.}{— Lloyd N. Trefethen}

The **SVD** is one of the most powerful tools in applied 
linear algebra.

content.title: Singular Value Decomposition
content.content:
Any $m \times n$ matrix $A$ can be written as:
\[
A = U\Sigma V\transpose
\]
where:

- $U$ is $m \times m$ orthogonal (left singular vectors)

- $V$ is $n \times n$ orthogonal (right singular vectors)

- $\Sigma$ is $m \times n$ diagonal with nonnegative 
 **singular values** $\sigma_1 \geq \sigma_2 \geq \cdots \geq 0$
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
**Low-rank approximation:** Keep only the largest singular values 
for compression.

**Principal directions:** Columns of $V$ give principal directions; 
singular values give magnitudes.

marginNotes.text:
Where were you when I laid the foundation
